oneOf.description: Turn a channel on (unmute) or off (mute).

AudioSetRequest.description: Request: mutate one audio channel's grant or level in the guest session.

AudioSetResponse.description: Response: updated channel state after the mutation.

AudioStatusRequest.description: Request: query current guest audio policy state.

ExecListEntry.description: One detached exec record summary. Discloses only bounded, non-sensitive metadata: the opaque id, slot, lifecycle state, create time, an argv hash (never the raw argv), and retained-log accounting.

ExecListRequest.description: Read-only listing of detached execs visible to the same VM token on the same guest boot. Carries no exec id, argv, or output; the boot id binds the request to the current guest generation.

GuestAudioChannelState.description: Current state of one audio channel as seen by the guest PipeWire session.

muted.description: True when the channel is muted (grant = off).

oneOf.description: Read current audio policy state from the guest PipeWire session. Advertised only when guestd is configured with a PipeWire control path.
Mutate mic/speaker grant or level in the guest PipeWire session. Advertised alongside `AudioStatus`; requires the same configuration.
Guest PipeWire session is unavailable or not configured.
Requested audio channel is not recognised.
Audio level value is out of the 0–100 range.
PipeWire enforcement call succeeded but the policy could not be applied.

GuestFileId.description: Closed set of host-declared guest files readable via `ReadGuestFile`. Currently ships only `GuestConfig` (the in-guest editable config working copy).

ReadGuestFileRequest.description: Framework read of a host-declared guest file, keyed by a closed enum (NOT a free-form path). guestd maps the key to the host-declared target.

ReadGuestFileResponse.description: Single-shot bounded response. `content` is capped at `READ_GUEST_FILE_MAX_BYTES`; oversize files fail with `FileTooLarge` BEFORE any read/allocation. `size_bytes`/`sha256` are guest-reported diagnostics only — the host recomputes both from the received bytes.

UsbipImportRequest.description: Guest-side USBIP lifecycle operation. `host` and `bus_id` are host-declared values resolved by d2bd from the trusted manifest; guestd validates both before invoking `usbip`.

UsbipStatusRequest.description: Side-effect-free guest USBIP import observation. Optional filters are validated with the same closed host/bus-id rules as `UsbipImport`.
